mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures consumer demand momentum, which drives S&P 500 earnings expectations and produces positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS monthly changes show Rank IC below 0.03 for 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a contractionary supply-labor shock that compresses profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines from prior month and UNRATE rises from prior month
failure_condition: The interaction term INDPRO times UNRATE fails to achieve an absolute t-statistic greater than 2.0 in predicting 20-day returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) during late-cycle expansions signals an imminent recession, causing equity risk premia to expand and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: During late-cycle expansions, UNRATE increases of more than 0.2 percentage points are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1400 thousand units signals a contraction in residential construction and durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1400 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals immediate labor market contraction, triggering risk-off repositioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month PAYEMS declines are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) captures the real cost of money; higher combined levels tighten financial conditions disproportionately and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term FEDFUNDS times CPIAUCSL does not significantly predict negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 2.0 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an active monetary tightening step that immediately raises short-term discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month FEDFUNDS increases are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 55% of instances.
